articles.title: Trump regulator splits GOP with prediction market fight
articles.summary: A Trump-appointed regulator is causing internal Republican conflict over how to handle prediction market regulation. The GOP split signals uncertainty about whether prediction markets will face stricter oversight or get the deregulatory treatment that could supercharge the industry.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets Are Created
articles.summary: Prediction markets don't just appear out of thin air,someone has to decide what questions to ask, set the rules, and bootstrap initial liquidity to get traders interested. The creation process determines which events get the spotlight and betting volume, making market makers the quiet gatekeepers of what the crowd gets to predict.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Are All the Rage, but This AI Stock Is a Much Better Investment
articles.summary: Some finance writer thinks buying AI stocks beats betting on prediction markets right now. The take matters because it shows mainstream media still doesn't grasp that prediction markets aren't just another asset class to trade against - they're information discovery engines that could actually help pick better AI investments.

articles.title: Bettors wagered $54 million on Khamenei’s death. Now they’re not getting paid.
articles.summary: Prediction market bettors placed $54 million in wagers on Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei dying, but the platforms are now refusing to pay out winnings. This highlights how geopolitical death markets create massive payout disputes when platforms decide the resolution criteria weren't clearly met or the event verification becomes too complicated.

articles.title: Wall Street Regulators Advance Crypto, Prediction Market Plans
articles.summary: Wall Street regulators are pushing forward new frameworks that would bring both cryptocurrency and prediction markets under clearer federal oversight. This signals Washington is finally ready to legitimize prediction markets as a real financial product, which could unlock institutional money and mainstream adoption for platforms that have operated in regulatory gray zones.

articles.title: Life After Congress: A prediction market duo
articles.summary: Two former members of Congress are launching their own prediction market platform, betting their political insider knowledge translates to better forecasting products. Their DC connections could give them an edge in attracting high-profile political bets and navigating the regulatory maze that's killed other platforms.

articles.title: Jake Paul's Betr Brings Polymarket To One Million Sports Bettors
articles.summary: Jake Paul's sports betting platform Betr is partnering with Polymarket to expose its one million users to prediction markets beyond traditional sports wagers. This marks the biggest mainstream crossover yet for crypto prediction markets, potentially bringing serious liquidity and legitimacy to political and event betting that has mostly lived in DeFi corners.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Are Eroding Our Civic Soul
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets are turning civic engagement into a cold, transactional betting game that strips away the moral and emotional investment democracy requires. If this critique gains traction, it could fuel regulatory crackdowns or public backlash that limits where and how political betting operates.

articles.title: How Is Kalshi Not Gambling?
articles.summary: Kalshi has managed to operate as a regulated derivatives exchange while offering markets that look suspiciously like sports betting, raising questions about where exactly the line falls between prediction markets and gambling. Their regulatory gymnastics could determine whether other platforms can follow the same playbook or if regulators will crack down on what they see as betting dressed up in financial market clothing.

articles.title: Polymarket and Kalshi’s Iran War Wagers Are Ghoulish — and Troubling
articles.summary: Prediction markets are catching heat for letting users bet on whether Iran and Israel will go to war, with critics calling the markets ghoulish profiteering on human suffering. The backlash signals growing regulatory and public relations pressure that could force platforms to pull geopolitical conflict bets, shrinking the tradeable universe just as these markets gain mainstream attention.

articles.title: DraftKings Is Using Its Sports-Betting Playbook to Win Prediction Markets Race
articles.summary: DraftKings is leveraging its user acquisition tactics, marketing muscle, and sports betting infrastructure to dominate the emerging prediction markets space. The move signals that mainstream gambling platforms see real money in political and event betting, which could bring massive liquidity and legitimacy to prediction markets while potentially crushing smaller pure-play competitors.

articles.title: Rep. Crenshaw odds: Will Trump’s snub spark an upset in TX-02?
articles.summary: Trump apparently snubbed Rep. Dan Crenshaw in Texas's 2nd district, and now bettors are wondering if this creates an opening for a primary challenger to knock off the Navy SEAL-turned-congressman. The drama matters because Crenshaw was once seen as a lock for reelection, but Trump's cold shoulder could shift the odds and create value for anyone willing to bet against the incumbent.
